data.body:
COMPLETE SESSION LOG — workflow visual editor remediation + PydanticAI cutover.

=== 1. EDITOR (story 744b / toxic-almond-datum — CLOSED+signed) ===
Goal: a human can view+edit a workflow IR visually and round-trip to the IR. Delivered +
browser-verified. Key work (commits on main):
- Sample: .rebar/workflows/roundtrip-demo.yaml (gitignored copy for convenience) + a TRACKED
  fixture tests/e2e/fixtures/roundtrip-demo.yaml (the test source of truth).
- BUG (19d3985d): branch then/else arms were encoded with '@' ids = illegal BPMN NCName ->
  real bpmn-moddle DROPS them -> Save rejected ("'then' is a required property"). Fixed:
  '.'-separated ids + <rebar:Config _role> markers; arms reconstructed STRUCTURALLY (gateway
  flow + role), id-independent. Found because unit tests used permissive xml.etree, not the
  real parser.
- Editor was DEAD at runtime (found via Playwright): (a) properties panel crashed —
  KindEntry didn't pass the `debounce` service -> TypeError -> killed selection + groups;
  fixed (a2be0572). (b) single column / no arrows — bpmn-auto-layout stacks our
  event-free/nested IR vertically with 0 edges; DROPPED it and rewrote bpmn.py _emit_di as a
  real layered layout (longest-path x, stacked y, docked edge waypoints, EXPANDED
  sub-processes) (2c66b1c7).
- Semantics (3355c92b): start/end events (entry indicator); branch gateway labeled with its
  `when`; flow labels true/false + else=default-flow slash; "after" label on the post-branch
  continuation (branches are BINARY then/else; the 3rd line is the needs-continuation, not a
  3rd outcome); agent prompt TEXT shown read-only (resolve_prompts in editor.py, works for
  built-in reviewers AND project .rebar/prompts); branch `when` field; config-format hint.
- Data integrity (3bd6e760): generic bpmn:task -> scripted (don't silently drop a drawn
  node); bpmn_to_ir RAISES on an unmappable element (loud Save failure, not silent drop);
  unique arm names "<gw> ▸ then/else".
- Connection labels + morph guard (438cb0a5): morph (change element type) PRESERVES
  <rebar:Config> — verified in-browser (the OSS-research-warned drop does NOT happen with our
  registered moddle descriptor); added a browser guard.
- Front-end: vendored bundle src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/ (bpmn-js +
  bpmn-js-properties-panel; built to dist/, committed, served LOCALLY — no CDN, no runtime
  npm). pyproject force-includes dist, excludes node_modules.
- TESTS: tests/e2e/ — faithful bpmn-moddle serialization tier (test_editor_e2e.py) + real
  browser Playwright tier (test_editor_browser.py) + Node harnesses (tests/e2e/js/). Self-
  skip without Node/Chromium. Plus offline unit tests. This tier exists because editor code
  that merely syntax-checked shipped BROKEN; the browser is the only faithful oracle.
- DOCS: docs/workflow-editor.md (developer/author audience; states clients need none of it),
  README + architecture.md updated.

=== 2. COMPLETION VERIFIER hardening ===
- (1d19c3e2) Graceful pass when there's nothing to verify: empty/placeholder/junk tickets
  ('test'/'asdf'/vague prose) made the verifier rabbit-hole (no criteria -> unbounded
  explore -> blew step budget). Now it makes a brief bounded effort and returns PASS with a
  note (prompt completion_verifier.md + instructions in completion.py). Verified live: a
  'test asdf placeholder' ticket PASSes in ~11s.
- _VERIFY_MIN_STEPS doubled 120->240; confirmed .rebar/config.conf sets NO max_steps override.
- (d07272a4) .rebar/ is GITIGNORED -> the demo workflow + prompts aren't in the repo, so the
  e2e demo test silently SKIPPED in CI and the verifier couldn't see them. Moved the e2e
  dependency to a tracked fixture so it runs in CI.
- Process note (USER DIRECTIVE): do NOT --force-close tickets; fix the gate instead. The
  gate (verify.require_completion_verification_for_close=true) runs the verifier on close;
  closed sparse-smelt-prime + riot-guy-flesh THROUGH the gate (signed). The verifier model
  defaults to sonnet (opus over-explores); REBAR_LLM_MODEL unset.

=== 3. PYDANTIC AI CUTOVER (story d6d1 / worn-mire-lath — IN PROGRESS) ===
Rigorous root-cause process (gather evidence -> subagent root-causes w/ falsifiable
experiments -> confirm -> fix RED->GREEN -> code review):
- BLOCKER A1 (66f2f910): PydanticAIRunner never passed usage_limits -> ignored
  cfg.max_iterations, fell back to pydantic-ai default request_limit=50 -> UsageLimitExceeded
  on heavy reviews. Proven offline (max_iterations=5 still tripped at 50). Fixed:
  UsageLimits(request_limit=ceil(max_iterations/2)) on every run_sync (the /2 reconciles
  langgraph super-steps ~2/cycle vs pydantic-ai requests ~1/cycle), wrap UsageLimitExceeded
  -> LLMRunnerError. A2 ("runaway") REJECTED — converges once budgeted.
- BLOCKER B1 (66f2f910): review_ticket/review_code had no step-budget floor (unlike
  completion's 240) -> default 25 (~12 tool calls) tripped the cap. Fixed:
  _REVIEW_MIN_STEPS=120 in both (operator-higher wins). B2 (langgraph over-explores) real but
  MOOT (langgraph removed).
- BLOCKER (verify_completion, 0011ca67): crashed on pydantic_ai with "'StructuredTool' has no
  '__name__'" — completion injected LANGCHAIN extra_tools (=_readonly_ticket_tools) which the
  pydantic_ai runner appended raw. Found by the THOROUGH live E2E (user-requested). Fixed:
  extra_tools=None for pydantic_ai (it has native show_ticket via pai_tools.rebar_tools);
  output_strategy=extract is langgraph-only and pydantic_ai's structured stack is the natural
  equivalent. Re-validated live.
- LIVE E2E suite tests/external/test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py (external, self-skipping):
  validates pydantic_ai across review_ticket(opus), review_code, scan_spec, verify_completion,
  text mode, workflow agent step. 6/6 PASS — before AND after the stack removal.
- FLIP (84d3516b): default runner derivation langgraph -> pydantic_ai. De-change-detector'd
  the runner tests (hardcoded runner=='langgraph' default assertions were change-detectors;
  rewrote to EV-4 invariance + dispatch contract get_runner(cfg).name==cfg.runner). USER
  INSIGHT: tests hardcoding the default runner are change-detectors.
- REMOVAL (2e9bd420): deleted LangGraphRunner/DeepAgentsRunner + all langchain-only helpers
  from runner.py/fs_tools.py/completion.py (-1282 lines); RUNNERS=("pydantic_ai","fake");
  available_backends/agents_extra_installed/_optional probe -> pydantic_ai. Kept the
  pydantic_ai path + shared fs helpers (pai_tools reuses _safe_path/_git_tracked/etc.) +
  shared findings pipeline. Tests rewritten BEHAVIORALLY; deleted langgraph-only live test.
  Code-reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NITS. Offline green; live E2E 6/6 post-removal.
- REMAINING for d6d1 (NOT done — next session): (a) remove langchain*/langgraph*/deepagents/
  langfuse from pyproject [agents]; (b) re-home tracing to a [tracing] OTel->Langfuse
  exporter, WIRED but UNVERIFIED (no-op without keys) per user; (c) docs/llm-framework.md +
  fix the get_prompt-vs-git-canonical drift; (d) update optionality CI ban-list/matrix. d6d1
  / worn-mire-lath stays OPEN until these land. Code-review nits to address: stale
  langchain/deepagents wording in _optional.py + a few test error-message strings; no test
  asserts REBAR_MCP_READONLY->withholding through the pydantic_ai runner (low sev).

=== 4. TICKET STATE ===
- Epic hump-seam-spice (e6d9, workflow-engine-v2): OPEN. 744b CLOSED. Only remaining open
  child = d6d1/worn-mire-lath (cutover: code done+validated; deps/tracing/docs remain). The
  epic closes once d6d1 finishes.
- NEW remediation epic: rub-hope-smear (da27-c916-f04e-4885) — prompt library/contracts/MCP
  steps/editor-UX brainstorm (PLACEHOLDER, design via /joe-brainstorm). Has the full agenda +
  context + relevant files in its description. roomy-spine-mirth parented under it.
- Closed THROUGH the gate (signed): sparse-smelt-prime (@-id bug), riot-guy-flesh (e2e story).

=== 5. KEY DECISIONS ===
- Editor layout: own Python layered layout, NOT bpmn-auto-layout (it stacks our event-free,
  nested IR in one column with no edges). Branch connections via LABELS, not synthetic
  gateways (round-trip fragility). Branches binary; "after" continuation.
- Tracing: lands wired-but-unverified (no-op without keys) per user.
- Cutover gated on LIVE validation BEFORE removal (the thorough E2E found a real blocker).
- Tests: behavioral, never change-detectors (esp. hardcoded default-runner assertions).
- Built-in prompts != reviewers (reviewers are a subset) — partially corrected for the editor
  display + flagged; the full prompt-library architecture is the remediation epic.

=== 6. ENVIRONMENT (IMPORTANT for next session) ===
- Use .venv/bin/rebar for dev + close-gate ops: it's nava-rebar installed EDITABLE (points at
  src/rebar) WITH the agents stack. The `rebar` on PATH is the pipx-distributed OLD v1 build
  (rejects schema_version 2). The completion gate, verify-completion, etc. must run via .venv.
- A CONCURRENT grounding job is active in the same tree (commits landing throughout; touches
  pyproject.toml, src/rebar/_optional.py, src/rebar/grounding/, tests/unit/grounding/,
  tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py). The lone offline-suite failure
  (test_external_isolation::test_no_external_marked_test_outside_external_dir) is caused by
  the grounding job's tests/unit/grounding/test_deps.py (contains the 'external' marker
  string) — PRE-EXISTING, not from this work. Coordinate pyproject edits.
- tests/e2e needs Node + a one-time npm install in tests/e2e/js + editor_assets, and
  Playwright Chromium for the browser tier; all self-skip when absent.
- Standing permission: commit directly to main. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is present (live calls work
  via .venv).
